meta.title: Original Ray Optics and Optical Instruments Practice Question Bank
meta.chapter: NCERT Class 12 Physics Chapter 9 - Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
meta.licenseNote: Original educational questions generated for this project. No copied coaching material or copyrighted question banks.

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Mirror Formula?
questions.solution: Mirror Formula: Relates object, image and focal length for spherical mirror. Therefore the correct relation is 1/v + 1/u = 1/f.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Mirror Magnification?
questions.answer: m = -v/u
questions.solution: Mirror Magnification: Image height ratio for mirror. Therefore the correct relation is m = -v/u.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: n1 sin i = n2 sin r
It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Snell Law?
questions.answer: n1 sin i = n2 sin r
questions.solution: Snell Law: Refraction at interface. Therefore the correct relation is n1 sin i = n2 sin r.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: n1 sin i = n2 sin r
It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Refractive Index?
questions.answer: n = c/v
questions.solution: Refractive Index: Higher refractive index means lower speed. Therefore the correct relation is n = c/v.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Critical Angle?
questions.answer: sin C = n2/n1
questions.solution: Critical Angle: Boundary condition for TIR. Therefore the correct relation is sin C = n2/n1.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Prism Deviation?
questions.answer: delta = i + e - A
questions.solution: Prism Deviation: Deviation by prism geometry. Therefore the correct relation is delta = i + e - A.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Prism Minimum Deviation?
questions.answer: mu = sin((A+dm)/2)/sin(A/2)
questions.solution: Prism Minimum Deviation: Find refractive index from minimum deviation. Therefore the correct relation is mu = sin((A+dm)/2)/sin(A/2).
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Lens Formula?
questions.solution: Lens Formula: Thin lens image relation. Therefore the correct relation is 1/v - 1/u = 1/f.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Lens Magnification?
questions.answer: m = v/u
questions.solution: Lens Magnification: Lens image height ratio. Therefore the correct relation is m = v/u.
questions.commonTrap: No extra minus for lens in this convention.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Power of Lens?
questions.answer: P = 1/f
questions.solution: Power of Lens: Convergence strength of lens. Therefore the correct relation is P = 1/f.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Lens Power Combination?
questions.answer: P = P1 + P2
questions.solution: Lens Power Combination: Powers add for thin lenses in contact. Therefore the correct relation is P = P1 + P2.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Lens Maker Formula?
questions.solution: Lens Maker Formula: Focal length from material and curvature. Therefore the correct relation is 1/f = (n-1)(1/R1 - 1/R2).
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Simple Microscope?
questions.answer: M = 1 + D/f
questions.solution: Simple Microscope: Magnification for final image at near point. Therefore the correct relation is M = 1 + D/f.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Compound Microscope?
questions.answer: M = (L/f_o)(D/f_e)
questions.solution: Compound Microscope: Magnification product of objective and eyepiece. Therefore the correct relation is M = (L/f_o)(D/f_e).
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Telescope Magnification?
questions.answer: M = f_o/f_e
questions.solution: Telescope Magnification: Angular magnification in normal adjustment. Therefore the correct relation is M = f_o/f_e.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Telescope Tube Length?
questions.answer: L = f_o + f_e
questions.solution: Telescope Tube Length: Normal adjustment tube length. Therefore the correct relation is L = f_o + f_e.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable
